de.uka.ilkd.key.logic

Class PosInTerm

  • java.lang.Object
    • de.uka.ilkd.key.logic.PosInTerm

  • public final class PosInTerm
extends Object
    Describes the position within a term by a sequence of integers. To create a position
    • obtain the position object representing the top level position, which refers to the term as a whole.
    • use method down(int) to keep track of the current position. Pass as argument the position of the subterm to be taken. Attention: Each invocation creates a new position in term object which has to be used for further navigation. The original one remains unchanged.

      Constructors 
      Modifier Constructor and Description
      private PosInTerm()
      only used once to create the singleton for the top level position
      private PosInTerm(char[] positions, char size, boolean copy)
      creates an instance representing the position positions[0..size-1]

    • Method Summary

      All Methods Static Methods Instance Methods Concrete Methods 
      Modifier and Type Method and Description
      int depth()
      the depth of the sub term described by this position
      PosInTerm down(int i)
      returns the position for the i-th subterm of the subterm described by this position
      boolean equals(Object o) 
      PosInTerm firstN(int n)
      returns the position of the depth-n parent term
      int getIndex()
      equivalent to getIndexAt(depth() - 1)
      int getIndexAt(int i)
      returns the index of the subterm at depth i
      Term getSubTerm(Term t)
      navigate to the subterm described by this position and return it if the described position does not exist in the term an IndexOutOfBoundsException is thrown
      static PosInTerm getTopLevel()
      returns the instance representing the top level position
      int hashCode() 
      String integerList(IntIterator it)
      returns a comma separated list of integers enclosed in brackets (the list contains all integers in the order as returned by the iterator)
      boolean isTopLevel()
      true if the position describes the top level position, i.e., the term as a whole
      IntIterator iterator()
      iterates through all indices in top to bottom order
      static PosInTerm parseReverseString(String s)
      create a position from the string The string contains a comma separated list of integers.
      IntIterator reverseIterator()
      iterates through all indices in bottom to top order
      String toString() 
      PosInTerm up()
      returns the position of the enclosing term

    • Field Detail

      • TOP_LEVEL

        private static final PosInTerm TOP_LEVEL

      • positions

        private final char[] positions

      • size

        private final char size

      • hash

        private volatile char hash

      • copy

        private boolean copy

    • Constructor Detail

      • PosInTerm

        private PosInTerm()
        only used once to create the singleton for the top level position

      • PosInTerm

        private PosInTerm(char[] positions,
                  char size,
                  boolean copy)
        creates an instance representing the position positions[0..size-1]
        Parameters:
        positions - the integer array where each element describes the position to be taken (in top-to-bottom order)
        size - the size of the integer list (attention: might be shorter than the length of the position array)
        copy - indicates (i.e. true) if the position array has to be copied when going downwards

    • Method Detail

      • parseReverseString

        public static PosInTerm parseReverseString(String s)
        create a position from the string The string contains a comma separated list of integers. The position created from the string encapsulated the given list but in reverse order
        Parameters:
        s - the String with the list of integers to be interpreted as term indices in reverse order
        Returns:
        the PosInTerm encapsulating the integer list in reverse order

      • getTopLevel

        public static PosInTerm getTopLevel()
        returns the instance representing the top level position
        Returns:
        the top level position

      • depth

        public int depth()
        the depth of the sub term described by this position
        Returns:
        the term depth

      • isTopLevel

        public boolean isTopLevel()
        true if the position describes the top level position, i.e., the term as a whole
        Returns:
        true iff top level

      • up

        public PosInTerm up()
        returns the position of the enclosing term
        Returns:
        the position of the parent

      • firstN

        public PosInTerm firstN(int n)
        returns the position of the depth-n parent term
        Parameters:
        n - the integer specifying the length of the prefix
        Returns:
        the prefix of this position of length n
        Throws:
        IndexOutOfBoundsException - if n is greater than the depth of this position

      • down

        public PosInTerm down(int i)
        returns the position for the i-th subterm of the subterm described by this position
        Parameters:
        i - the index of the subterm
        Returns:
        the position of the i-th subterm

      • getIndexAt

        public int getIndexAt(int i)
        returns the index of the subterm at depth i
        Parameters:
        i - the depth of the subterm whose index it to be returned
        Returns:
        an int such that term.subAt(this).sub(getIndex(i)) == term.subAt(firstN(i+1))

      • getIndex

        public int getIndex()
        equivalent to getIndexAt(depth() - 1)
        Returns:
        the index of the subterm described by this position

      • getSubTerm

        public Term getSubTerm(Term t)
        navigate to the subterm described by this position and return it if the described position does not exist in the term an IndexOutOfBoundsException is thrown
        Parameters:
        t - the Term
        Returns:
        the sub term of term t at this position
        Throws:
        an - IndexOutOfBoundsException if no subterm exists at this position

        public int hashCode()
        Overrides:
        hashCode in class Object

        public boolean equals(Object o)
        Overrides:
        equals in class Object

      • integerList

        public String integerList(IntIterator it)
        returns a comma separated list of integers enclosed in brackets (the list contains all integers in the order as returned by the iterator)
        Parameters:
        it - the iterator
        Returns:
        the String with the list of integers

      • iterator

        public IntIterator iterator()
        iterates through all indices in top to bottom order
        Returns:
        iterator through all indices

      • reverseIterator

        public IntIterator reverseIterator()
        iterates through all indices in bottom to top order
        Returns:
        iterator through all indices in reverse order

        public String toString()
        Overrides:
